key_insight: POO-LPSP replaces the standard eigenvector-based AHP priority derivation with a parallel metaheuristic optimizer that solves revised penalty-squared formulations for more robust priority extraction.
engineering_relevance: It offers a computationally practical way to derive decision priorities in large or difficult AHP problems, such as vendor selection, where exact non-linear optimization is otherwise burdensome.
method: Reformulates AHP priority derivation as revised LPSP optimization models (LPPDS and LPPWS).
Minimizes revised inconsistency measures: RMPSV and RMPSWV.
Uses Parallel Osprey Optimization Algorithm (POOA) to efficiently solve the non-linear models.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on a numerical Generative AI vendor selection case study, showing practical utility and computational efficiency.
watch_item: As a metaheuristic approach, results may depend on tuning, convergence behavior, and repeatability across different pairwise comparison matrices.
paper_title: POO-LPSP: Parallel Osprey Optimized Least Penalty-Squared Prioritization Methods for Priority Derivation in the Analytic Hierarchy Process
